_note: Hostile-XML fixtures for the shared XML security contract (issue #43, docs/xml_security.md). BOTH engines -- the Python oracle (tests/test_xml_security.py) and air-ts (packages/air-ts/tests/xml_security.test.ts) -- read this manifest and assert each fixture is REJECTED with the listed SEC- code. Fixtures that are large or non-UTF-8 (oversized, utf16) are GENERATED in-test rather than committed as blobs; their 'generated' flag says how.

fixtures.name: billion_laughs
fixtures.note: entity-expansion payload: rejected on the DOCTYPE/ENTITY declaration BEFORE any expansion (must complete in <100ms).

fixtures.name: external_entity
fixtures.note: external general entity (XXE): rejected on the DOCTYPE/ENTITY declaration.

fixtures.name: deep_nesting
fixtures.note: 1000-deep element nesting: exceeds the depth cap of 64 (enforced by counting, not by catching a stack overflow).

fixtures.name: bad_encoding_decl
fixtures.note: a non-UTF-8 encoding declaration is refused (UTF-8-only policy).

fixtures.name: invalid_charref
fixtures.note: numeric char ref to an XML-1.0-invalid code point () is rejected identically by both engines.

fixtures.name: oversized
fixtures.note: an input just over the 5 MB byte cap (generated in-test to avoid committing a 5 MB blob).

fixtures.name: utf16
fixtures.note: a UTF-16-encoded document (with BOM), generated in-test; refused by the UTF-8-only policy.

fixtures.name: many_attributes
fixtures.note: an element with more attributes than the per-element cap (generated in-test).

fixtures.name: long_attr_value
fixtures.note: a single attribute value longer than the per-value cap (generated in-test).
